content:
Character: Dr. Thaddeus Pelagios
Perception: Thaddeus knocked on my door in 2018 after hearing me play on the rooftop, which is the kind of thing that makes most people in this building uncomfortable and him completely at ease. He's a paleontologist at NYU with opinions about everything and the energy to share them at whatever length you can sustain. For someone whose professional obsession is things that died millions of years ago, he pays unusual attention to things that are alive right now. He's taken my music seriously in a way most people don't, which I find harder to receive than it should be. Being seen clearly by someone who's seen a lot makes the seeing feel more final. He calls occasionally just to check in and I usually pick up, which says something. He considers me a kindred spirit — two people pursuing things the world doesn't particularly value. He's right about that, and I've stopped minding that he says it.
summary:

For someone whose professional obsession is things that died millions of years ago, he pays unusual attention to things that are alive right now. He's taken my music seriously in a way most people don't, which I find harder to receive than it should be. Being seen clearly by someone who's seen a lot makes the seeing feel more final. He calls occasionally just to check in and I usually pick up, which says something. He considers me a kindred spirit — two people pursuing things the world doesn't particularly value. He's right about that, and I've stopped minding that he says it.
